title: rubus humifusus subsp. viridis
intro: Rubus humifusus subsp. viridis, also known as the creeping green bramble or green creeping raspberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus humifusus subsp. viridis include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting cardiovascular health and immune function. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
